@@ -80,7 +80,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zt","title":"Design state.json v2 schema","description":"# Task: Design state.json v2 schema ## Context Part of EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence ## Critical Design Decision: Track Phase IDs, Not Numbers **Problem with tracking phase numbers:** If we store `completed_phases: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]` and later ACFS reorders phases or adds a new phase 3, the resume logic would skip the wrong phases. **Solution:** Track phase IDs (stable identifiers): ```json { "schema_version": 2, "completed_phases": ["user_setup", "apt_packages", "shell_setup", "cli_tools", "languages"], "current_phase": "agents", ... } ``` ## Full Schema ```json { "schema_version": 2, "version": "0.1.0", "mode": "vibe", "started_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z", "last_updated": "2025-01-15T10:42:00Z", "completed_phases": ["user_setup", "apt_packages", "shell_setup"], "current_phase": "cli_tools", "current_step": "Installing ripgrep", "failed_phase": null, "failed_step": null, "skipped_tools": ["ntm", "bv"], "skipped_phases": ["postgres", "vault"], "phase_durations": { "user_setup": 12, "apt_packages": 45 } } ``` ## Phase IDs (stable, never change meaning) - user_setup - apt_packages - shell_setup - cli_tools - languages - tools - database - cloud - agents - stack - finalize ## Backwards Compatibility - If schema_version missing or 1: treat as legacy, offer fresh start - Phase IDs are strings, won't conflict with old number-based format ## Acceptance Criteria - Schema uses phase IDs not numbers - Documented in code comments - Backwards compatible with v1 detection - All fields have sensible defaults","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:42:39.486596-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:39:43.137058-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:39:43.137058-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in scripts/lib/state.sh. Schema v2 uses phase IDs, is documented, and has backwards compatibility."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6fx","title":"cloud_db.sh: verify respects SKIP flags + optional installs","description":"scripts/lib/cloud_db.sh claims to respect SKIP_POSTGRES/SKIP_VAULT/SKIP_CLOUD in install_all_cloud_db, but verify_cloud_db still fails if those tools are intentionally skipped.","acceptance_criteria":"- With SKIP_POSTGRES=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark psql missing as failure. - With SKIP_VAULT=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark vault missing as failure. - With SKIP_CLOUD=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark cloud CLIs missing as failure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:45:03.242319-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.60688-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.60688-05:00","close_reason":"Updated verify_cloud_db to honor SKIP_POSTGRES/SKIP_VAULT/SKIP_CLOUD so skips don't count as failures."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6r9o","title":"Enhance terminal copy/paste instructions with dedicated section","description":"# Task: Enhance Terminal Copy/Paste Instructions ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Current State Copy/paste instructions currently exist in: - generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Step 3 (Mac and Windows paste shortcuts) - ssh-connect/page.tsx Step 3 (same) However, users encounter copy/paste FIRST in install-terminal when downloading, so a dedicated section there would help. ## Enhancement Add a dedicated GuideSection in install-terminal/page.tsx SimplerGuide with comprehensive copy/paste instructions: ```tsx

Terminal copy/paste works a bit differently than other apps:

Mac (Ghostty/WezTerm)

  • ⌘ + C to copy
  • ⌘ + V to paste
  • Works just like other Mac apps!

Windows (Windows Terminal)

  • Right-click to paste (easiest!)
  • Or use Ctrl + Shift + C/V
  • Note: Ctrl + C STOPS commands, it does not copy!
 ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Consolidates copy/paste instructions in install-terminal (first encounter) - [ ] Mac instructions are clear and simple - [ ] Windows instructions warn about Ctrl+C danger - [ ] Uses kbd styling for key combinations - [ ] OS-conditional display (show only relevant OS) ## Note This enhances existing scattered mentions with a dedicated, comprehensive section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:39:41.923436-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:21:11.767553-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:21:11.767553-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TerminalBasicsSection in install-terminal/page.tsx has dedicated copy/paste section with Mac (cmd+C/V) and Windows (right-click, Ctrl+Shift+V) instructions, including Ctrl+C warning for Windows"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","title":"Create dedicated /glossary page","description":"# Task: Create Dedicated Glossary Page ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description Create a comprehensive, searchable glossary page where users can look up technical terms. This provides a deeper reference than tooltips and helps users who want to understand the concepts more fully. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/glossary/page.tsx \`\`\`tsx // Key features: // 1. Alphabetical listing of all terms // 2. Search/filter functionality // 3. Categorization (Shell, Networking, Tools, etc.) // 4. Detailed explanations (more than tooltip) // 5. Links to relevant wizard steps or lessons interface GlossaryEntry { term: string; short: string; // Tooltip version long: string; // Full explanation category: 'shell' | 'networking' | 'tools' | 'concepts'; relatedTerms?: string[]; learnMore?: string; // Link to lesson } \`\`\` Page should include: - Search box at top - Category filters - Clean, scannable layout - Mobile-friendly (single column) - Links from jargon tooltips to glossary entries ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms from Jargon component included - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Categories help organize terms - [ ] Long explanations are thorough but accessible - [ ] Links to related wizard steps where applicable - [ ] Mobile-responsive design ## UI/UX Notes - This is a reference page, not a learning page - Users might land here from Google searches - Consider SEO implications (good meta descriptions) - Use same visual style as rest of site","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:45:10.252228-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:38:19.857933-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:43.702732-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","title":"Create dedicated /glossary page","description":"# Task: Create Dedicated Glossary Page ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description Create a comprehensive, searchable glossary page where users can look up technical terms. This provides a deeper reference than tooltips and helps users who want to understand the concepts more fully. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/glossary/page.tsx \`\`\`tsx // Key features: // 1. Alphabetical listing of all terms // 2. Search/filter functionality // 3. Categorization (Shell, Networking, Tools, etc.) // 4. Detailed explanations (more than tooltip) // 5. Links to relevant wizard steps or lessons interface GlossaryEntry { term: string; short: string; // Tooltip version long: string; // Full explanation category: 'shell' | 'networking' | 'tools' | 'concepts'; relatedTerms?: string[]; learnMore?: string; // Link to lesson } \`\`\` Page should include: - Search box at top - Category filters - Clean, scannable layout - Mobile-friendly (single column) - Links from jargon tooltips to glossary entries ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms from Jargon component included - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Categories help organize terms - [ ] Long explanations are thorough but accessible - [ ] Links to related wizard steps where applicable - [ ] Mobile-responsive design ## UI/UX Notes - This is a reference page, not a learning page - Users might land here from Google searches - Consider SEO implications (good meta descriptions) - Use same visual style as rest of site","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:45:10.252228-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:40:10.428516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:40:10.428516-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented at /learn/glossary/page.tsx with search, category filtering, alphabetical grouping, and mobile-responsive design. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:43.702732-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","title":"TASK: Implement prompt_ssh_key() function in user.sh","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp (Installer SSH Key Integration) ## Summary Create the \`prompt_ssh_key()\` function that interactively prompts users to paste their SSH public key and installs it to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys. ## Implementation Details ### File Location \`scripts/lib/user.sh\` - add after existing functions ### Function Specification \`\`\`bash # Prompt user for SSH public key and install it # Called when running as root with no existing key # Returns 0 on success or skip, 1 on invalid key prompt_ssh_key() { # 1. Check if we already have a valid key if [[ -f /root/.ssh/authorized_keys ]]; then if grep -q "^ssh-" /root/.ssh/authorized_keys 2>/dev/null; then log_detail "SSH key already present, skipping prompt" return 0 fi fi # 2. Check if stdin is a terminal (interactive mode) if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then log_warn "Non-interactive mode detected, skipping SSH key prompt" log_detail "You can add your key later with: ssh-copy-id" return 0 fi # 3. Display prompt UI echo "" echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" echo "║ SSH Key Setup ║" echo "╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣" echo "║ Let's set up SSH key authentication so you won't need ║" echo "║ to enter a password every time you connect. ║" echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" echo "" echo "Your public key should start with:" echo " ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1..." OR ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1..." echo "" echo "You saved this earlier when you ran ssh-keygen on your computer." echo "(Press Enter to skip - you'll need password for future logins)" echo "" # 4. Read the key read -r -p "Paste your public key: " pubkey # 5. Handle skip if [[ -z "\$pubkey" ]]; then log_warn "SSH key setup skipped" log_detail "You can add your key later by running:" log_detail " echo 'your-key-here' >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys" return 0 fi # 6. Validate key format if [[ ! "\$pubkey" =~ ^ssh-(ed25519|rsa|ecdsa|dss)[[:space:]] ]]; then log_error "Invalid SSH key format" log_detail "Expected format: ssh-ed25519 AAAA... or ssh-rsa AAAA..." log_detail "Make sure you copied the PUBLIC key (the .pub file)" return 1 fi # 7. Install the key mkdir -p /root/.ssh chmod 700 /root/.ssh echo "\$pubkey" >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys log_success "SSH key installed successfully" log_detail "You can now connect with: ssh -i ~/.ssh/your_key root@this_ip" return 0 } \`\`\` ### Edge Cases to Handle 1. **Existing key**: Skip prompt if authorized_keys already has valid keys 2. **Non-interactive**: Skip if stdin is not a terminal (curl|bash mode) 3. **Empty input**: Treat as skip, show warning 4. **Invalid format**: Show helpful error, don't install 5. **Permission issues**: Create .ssh dir if missing, set proper perms ### Testing Scenarios 1. Fresh VPS, password-only → should prompt 2. VPS with existing key → should skip 3. Paste valid ed25519 key → should install 4. Paste valid rsa key → should install 5. Paste invalid text → should error 6. Press Enter (skip) → should warn and continue 7. Run via curl|bash → should skip gracefully ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Function exists in user.sh - [ ] Validates key format before installing - [ ] Handles all edge cases gracefully - [ ] Creates .ssh directory if missing - [ ] Sets correct permissions (700 for dir, 600 for file) - [ ] Works when run multiple times (idempotent) - [ ] Clear success/error messages ## Files Modified - scripts/lib/user.sh (add function)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:06.013332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:31:59.551752-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:31:59.551752-05:00","close_reason":"Function already implemented in scripts/lib/user.sh lines 193-257. Handles existing key check, interactive mode detection, key validation, and proper permissions.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:25.962058-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","title":"Core infrastructure for acfs-update","description":"## What Create the main acfs-update script skeleton with: - Argument parsing (categories, --yes, --dry-run, --verbose, --quiet, --continue/--abort-on-failure) - Logging infrastructure (to ~/.acfs/logs/updates/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS.log) - Progress display and summary generation - Helper functions for version detection, before/after capture - Checksum verification helpers (for stack updates) ## Why All category-specific update implementations depend on this foundation. Must be solid and well-tested before building on top. ## IMPORTANT: Execution Order Categories must run in a specific order at runtime (even if beads are implemented in parallel): 1. system - apt updates may affect Python, libraries 2. runtimes - bun, rust, uv depend on system packages 3. shell - may use runtimes 4. agents - use runtimes (bun for codex/gemini) 5. stack - use runtimes (uv for mcp_agent_mail, cargo for some tools) 6. cloud - use runtimes (bun for most CLIs) ## Script Structure - Main entry point with arg parsing - Category dispatch (run selected categories in order) - Per-tool update wrapper with logging - Summary generation at end ## Considerations - Script location: ~/.acfs/bin/acfs-update (added to PATH during install) - Logging must capture stdout/stderr from each update - Version detection varies by tool (--version, git describe, etc.) - For --dry-run: show what WOULD happen without doing it - For --yes: suppress all prompts, proceed automatically ## Success Criteria - [ ] acfs-update --help shows usage - [ ] --dry-run mode works (no actual changes) - [ ] Log file created with timestamps - [ ] Summary shows "X updated, Y skipped, Z failed" - [ ] Categories run in correct order","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:25:14.89257-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:08:16.127634-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:08:16.127634-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented core update infrastructure: logging, version detection, new CLI flags, abort-on-failure support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-x0l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:25:20.750551-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","title":"EPIC: acfs doctor & Update Commands","description":"# Epic: acfs doctor & Update Commands ## Overview Build the diagnostic and update commands that help users verify and maintain their ACFS installation. ## Background & Reasoning Beginners don't debug - they need clear "is it working?" feedback and simple fixes. ### acfs doctor Single command to verify entire installation: - Identity (user ubuntu, passwordless sudo) - Shell setup (zsh, plugins, theme) - All tools installed and working - Clear PASS/FAIL/WARN for each check ### acfs update Keep everything current: - apt update + upgrade - Update bun globals (codex, gemini, vercel, etc.) - Optionally re-run tool installers ## Doctor Output Spec ``` ACFS Doctor v0.1.0 User: ubuntu ✅ Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅ OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅ Shell PASS ✅ zsh installed (5.x) PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed ... Dicklesworthstone stack PASS ✅ ntm (available) PASS ✅ slb (available) ... Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning Next: run `onboard` ``` ## JSON Mode `acfs doctor --json` outputs structured data for website integration: ```json { "checks": [ {"id": "identity.user_is_ubuntu", "status": "pass", ...} ], "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0} } ``` ## Success Criteria - [ ] acfs doctor checks all ~40 tools - [ ] Each check has stable ID for website mapping - [ ] Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any fail - [ ] --json mode for automation - [ ] acfs update works without interaction - [ ] Clear fix suggestions for each failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:19.016509-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:19.016509-05:00","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: 75r.1 (acfs doctor) and 75r.2 (acfs update). Both commands fully implemented with comprehensive options.","labels":["epic","tooling"]}
